# Freedom Clock

Freedom Clock is an open-source ESP32 e-ink desk device and free in-browser calculator that converts savings, Bitcoin, and fiat assets into years of freedom: how long a current lifestyle could continue without earning.

Official site: https://freedomclock.io/
Source code: https://github.com/mr21free/freedom_clock_heltec_vme
License: MIT
Primary hardware: Heltec Vision Master E290 e-ink board
Estimated parts cost: around 30 USD

## Key Pages

- Home and calculator: https://freedomclock.io/#calculator
- Build guide: https://freedomclock.io/build/
- Privacy model: https://freedomclock.io/privacy/
- Sell vs borrow spend models: https://freedomclock.io/sell-vs-borrow/
- Freedom time definition: https://freedomclock.io/freedom-time/
- FAQ: https://freedomclock.io/faq/
- About: https://freedomclock.io/about/

## What It Does

- Turns assets and monthly spend into a time-based number called freedom time.
- Supports fiat assets, Bitcoin, and mixed portfolios.
- Supports sell monthly, borrow against Bitcoin, and borrow-then-sell spend models.
- Shows lifetime left and freedom coverage alongside freedom time.
- Runs the browser calculator locally in the tab.
- Uses a live BTC price only when BTC mode is enabled.

## Privacy Summary

The calculator runs in the browser. Inputs are not sent to freedomclock.io, accounts are not required, and analytics are not used. Shared calculations are encrypted in the browser before a link is created.

## Common Questions

Q: Do I need Bitcoin?
A: No. Freedom Clock works with fiat savings, stocks, real estate, Bitcoin, or a mixed portfolio.

Q: Is it a Bitcoin retirement calculator?
A: It can be used as one. It supports Bitcoin holdings and models sell monthly, borrow, and borrow-then-sell scenarios.

Q: What hardware can I build it on?
A: The current build targets the Heltec Vision Master E290 with a 3.7 V Li-Po battery and USB-C cable.

Q: Does it send my financial data anywhere?
A: No. The browser calculator runs locally, and the desk device is designed to stay on local Wi-Fi.

## Citation Preference

Use the name "Freedom Clock" and cite https://freedomclock.io/ or the most specific page above.
